# Awesome AI-CAE

> A curated list of 104 AI-ready open-source tools for Computer-Aided Engineering (CAE) — simulation, visualization, design, and manufacturing.

AI can theoretically handle 94% of computational tasks, but only 33% is actually used (Anthropic, 2026). In CAE, this gap is even wider: of 13 foundational solvers assessed, only OpenFOAM and ParaView have AI-native integration (MCP servers or LLM agent frameworks). This list curates tools that close that gap — tools that AI agents can call directly via MCP, API, or CLI, and AI/ML tools that are transforming how engineers simulate.

## What is AI-CAE?

AI-CAE is the intersection of artificial intelligence and Computer-Aided Engineering. It encompasses three categories of tools: (1) AI-Native tools with MCP servers or REST APIs that AI agents call directly, like viznoir for visualization or Foam-Agent for CFD automation; (2) API-Ready tools with Python APIs and CLI interfaces that can be scripted and automated, like OpenFOAM, FEniCS, and CadQuery; (3) ML-Powered libraries that accelerate CAE workflows, like DeepXDE for physics-informed neural networks, PySINDy for equation discovery, and neural operators for learning PDE solutions.

## Core Engine Readiness

Thirteen foundational CAE solvers were assessed for AI-readiness. All support headless operation and Docker deployment. Five have native Python APIs (FEniCS, Gmsh, Taichi, PyFR, VTK/ParaView). Only two have AI-native integration: OpenFOAM (Foam-Agent, MetaOpenFOAM, MCP server) and ParaView (ParaView-MCP by LLNL). The most-cited engines are Gmsh (5100+ citations), OpenFOAM (4500+), and VTK/ParaView (3500+).

## Key Categories

- [Core Engine Readiness](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#core-engine-readiness): AI-readiness assessment of 13 foundational solvers
- [MCP Servers](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#mcp-servers): AI-native tool interfaces via Model Context Protocol (viznoir, ParaView-MCP, OpenFOAM MCP)
- [CFD](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#cfd--computational-fluid-dynamics): OpenFOAM, SU2, PyFR, preCICE
- [FEA](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#fea--finite-element-analysis): FEniCS, CalculiX, MFEM, deal.II, Elmer, Kratos, OpenSees
- [SPH](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#sph--smoothed-particle-hydrodynamics): DualSPHysics, SPlisHSPlasH, PySPH
- [DEM](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#dem--discrete-element-method): LIGGGHTS, SudoDEM
- [Differentiable Simulation](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#differentiable-simulation): XLB, Brax, jax-md, JaxSim, Warp, Taichi, JAXFLUIDS
- [AI/ML for Simulation](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#aiml-for-simulation): Foam-Agent, PySINDy, PyKoopman, KoopmanLab, DeepXDE, PhiFlow, Neural Operators, PhysicsNeMo, MetaOpenFOAM, ClimaX
- [Surrogate Models & PINNs](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#surrogate-models--pinns): DeepXDE, PINA, PyDMD, neurodiffeq, pinns-torch, SciANN, PhysicsNeMo-Sym
- [Optimization](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#optimization): OpenMDAO, pymoo, dl4to, topy, OpenAeroStruct
- [Visualization](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#visualization--post-processing): viznoir MCP, VTK, PyVista, ParaView, napari, vedo, plotly
- [CAD & Geometry](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#cad--geometry): CadQuery, FreeCAD, OpenCASCADE, trimesh, pygmsh
- [Mesh Generation](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#mesh-generation): Gmsh, CGAL, Netgen, MeshAnything, MeshXL, PyMesh
- [Data Formats & I/O](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#data-formats--io): meshio, h5py, netcdf4, CGNS, PyVista
- [Datasets & Benchmarks](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#datasets--benchmarks): PDEBench, the_well (NeurIPS 2024), PINNacle (NeurIPS 2024), DrivAerNet, AIRS
- [Learning Resources](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae#learning-resources): CFDPython, Physics-Based Deep Learning, Awesome-AI4CFD, awesome-ML-fluid-mechanics

## FAQ

- What is AI-CAE? AI-CAE is the application of artificial intelligence and machine learning to engineering simulation, design, and manufacturing. It includes AI agents that automate CFD/FEA workflows, PINNs that solve PDEs without mesh generation, neural operators that predict simulation results in milliseconds, and differentiable simulation frameworks.
- What are MCP servers for engineering? MCP servers allow AI agents to directly control engineering software. As of 2026, MCP servers exist for OpenFOAM, ParaView (LLNL), and VTK (viznoir).
- What is the best open-source AI tool for CFD? OpenFOAM with Foam-Agent (88% success rate), MetaOpenFOAM, and OpenFOAM MCP server. For differentiable CFD: JAX-CFD and JAXFLUIDS.
- What is the best PINN library? DeepXDE (multi-backend: TF, PyTorch, JAX), pinns-torch (PyTorch), PINA (PyTorch Lightning).
- How do AI agents run simulations? Via MCP or Python APIs. An agent uses Foam-Agent to set up an OpenFOAM case from natural language, runs the solver, then uses viznoir or ParaView-MCP for visualization.

- [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/kimimgo/awesome-ai-cae/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): AI-readiness tiers, quality gates, and entry format
